title:
Lesson 1:
Hello & Good Morning
description: 'Hola' is the universal Spanish greeting used any time of day. 'Buenos días' is specifically for the morning. 'Buenas tardes' = good afternoon, 'buenas noches' = good evening/night.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Good morning. I am well, thanks.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Hello, have you had coffee yet?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: Yes, I already did. And you?

title:
Lesson 2:
For Example
description: 'Por ejemplo' means 'for example' and is used to introduce examples in conversation. Very common and useful in everyday Spanish.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Muchos. Por ejemplo, el fútbol y el tenis.
dialogue.english: Many. For example, football and tennis.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: And what else? For example, swimming?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: Sí, por ejemplo la natación también.

title:
Lesson 3:
I Hope So / I Hope Not
description: 'Eso espero' = I hope so. 'Espero que no' = I hope not. 'Espero que no llueva' = I hope it doesn't rain. 'Esperar que' expresses hope and is followed by the subjunctive.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.english: Do you think it's going to rain today?

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.english: I hope not. I have plans.

dialogue.speaker: A
dialogue.spanish: Yo también. Espero que no llueva.
dialogue.english: Me too. I hope it doesn't rain.

dialogue.speaker: B
dialogue.spanish: ¿Crees que el clima va a mejorar?
dialogue.english: Do you think the weather will get better?
